---
title: 'ISA 401 Project: CFB Recruiting'
author: "Andrew Wolfson"
date: "2026-04-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# 0. SETUP
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rvest)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

api_key <- Sys.getenv("CFBD_API_KEY")
```


```{r}
# 1. API FUNCTION FOR GAME DATA

get_games <- function(year) {
  url <- paste0("https://api.collegefootballdata.com/games?year=", year)
  
  res <- GET(
    url,
    add_headers(Authorization = paste("Bearer", api_key))
  )
  
  message("Year: ", year, " | Status: ", status_code(res))
  
  Sys.sleep(0.5)
  
  fromJSON(content(res, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), flatten = TRUE)
}
```


```{r}
# acquire/cache
dir.create("data", showWarnings = FALSE)
# Create a local data folder and cache raw API results.
# This avoids repeated API calls and makes the project reproducible if the API changes or rate limits. 


if (!file.exists("data/games_raw.rds")) {
  years <- 2010:2025
  games_raw <- map_dfr(years, get_games)
  saveRDS(games_raw, "data/games_raw.rds")
} else {
  games_raw <- readRDS("data/games_raw.rds")
}

# check column names
names(games_raw)
```

```{r}
# 3. CLEAN GAME DATA

# Keep only completed games involving at least one FBS team.
# removes incomplete games and non-relevant classifications before building team records.
games_clean <- games_raw %>%
  filter(
    completed == TRUE,
    homeClassification == "fbs" | awayClassification == "fbs"
  ) %>%
  select(
    season,
    week,
    seasonType,
    homeTeam,
    awayTeam,
    homePoints,
    awayPoints,
    homeClassification,
    awayClassification,
    notes
  )
```

```{r}
# 4. BUILD TEAM-LEVEL RECORDS

home_df <- games_clean %>%
  transmute(
    year = season,
    team = homeTeam,
    opponent = awayTeam,
    points_for = homePoints,
    points_against = awayPoints,
    point_diff = homePoints - awayPoints,
    win = ifelse(homePoints > awayPoints, 1, 0)
  )

away_df <- games_clean %>%
  transmute(
    year = season,
    team = awayTeam,
    opponent = homeTeam,
    points_for = awayPoints,
    points_against = homePoints,
    point_diff = awayPoints - homePoints,
    win = ifelse(awayPoints > homePoints, 1, 0)
  )

games_long <- bind_rows(home_df, away_df)

records_clean <- games_long %>%
  group_by(year, team) %>%
  summarise(
    games = n(),
    wins = sum(win, na.rm = TRUE),
    losses = games - wins,
    win_pct = round(wins / games, 3),
    points_for_avg = round(mean(points_for, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    points_against_avg = round(mean(points_against, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    avg_pd = round(mean(point_diff, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# 5. CLEAN TEAM NAME FUNCTION

# Standardize team names across sources before joining.
# handles inconsistent formatting
clean_team <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_to_upper() %>%
    str_replace_all("&", "AND") %>%
    str_replace_all("[^A-Z ]", "") %>%
    str_squish()
}

records_clean <- records_clean %>%
  mutate(team_clean = clean_team(team))
```

```{r}
# 6. SCRAPE / CACHE 247 RECRUITING DATA


# Use tryCatch so the scraping process does not fail completely if a page is unavailable.
get_247 <- function(year) {
  url <- paste0("https://247sports.com/season/", year, "-football/teamrankings/")
  
  message("Scraping 247 recruiting year: ", year)
  # If a page cannot be read, return an empty tibble for that year.
  page <- tryCatch(
    read_html(url),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  if (is.null(page)) {
    return(tibble())
  }
  
  Sys.sleep(1)
  
  rows <- page %>% html_elements(".wrapper")
  
  map_df(rows, function(x) {
    star_nodes <- x %>%
      html_element(".star-commits-list") %>%
      html_elements("li")
    
    stars_df <- map_df(star_nodes, function(node) {
      tibble(
        label = node %>% html_element("h2") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE),
        value = node %>% html_element("div") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE) %>% as.numeric()
      )
    })
    
    five_star <- stars_df %>% filter(str_detect(label, "5")) %>% pull(value)
    four_star <- stars_df %>% filter(str_detect(label, "4")) %>% pull(value)
    three_star <- stars_df %>% filter(str_detect(label, "3")) %>% pull(value)
    
    
    # Some teams may not have every star-category value listed.
    tibble(
      year = year,
      rec_rank = x %>% html_element(".primary") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE) %>% as.numeric(),
      team = x %>% html_element(".team") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE),
      five_star = ifelse(length(five_star) == 0, NA, five_star),
      four_star = ifelse(length(four_star) == 0, NA, four_star),
      three_star = ifelse(length(three_star) == 0, NA, three_star),
      rec_avg = x %>% html_element(".avg") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE) %>% as.numeric(),
      rec_points = x %>% html_element(".number") %>% html_text(trim = TRUE) %>% as.numeric()
    )
  })
}
# Missing star counts are stored as NA and later handled when calculating total commits.


if (!file.exists("data/recruiting_raw.rds")) {
  recruiting_raw <- map_dfr(2010:2025, get_247)
  saveRDS(recruiting_raw, "data/recruiting_raw.rds")
} else {
  recruiting_raw <- readRDS("data/recruiting_raw.rds")
}
# Clean recruiting data and calculate total commits.
recruiting_clean <- recruiting_raw %>%
  mutate( # rowSums prevents missing star categories from breaking the commit total.
    team_clean = clean_team(team),
    commits = rowSums(across(c(five_star, four_star, three_star)), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  select(
    year,
    team_clean,
    rec_rank,
    five_star,
    four_star,
    three_star,
    commits,
    rec_avg,
    rec_points
  )
```

```{r}
# 7. SCRAPE / CACHE AP POLL DATA

poll_map <- tribble(
  ~year, ~final_id, ~n_polls,
  2010, 1021, 16,
  2011, 1037, 16,
  2012, 1053, 16,
  2013, 1069, 16,
  2014, 1085, 16,
  2015, 1101, 16,
  2016, 1117, 16,
  2017, 1133, 16,
  2018, 1149, 16,
  2019, 1165, 16,
  2020, 1181, 16,
  2021, 1197, 16,
  2022, 1217, 16,
  2023, 1233, 16,
  2024, 1250, 17,
  2025, 1267, 17
)

# Use tryCatch to handle unavailable AP poll pages without stopping the full scrape.
get_ap_poll <- function(appollid, year, poll_num) {
  url <- paste0("https://www.collegepollarchive.com/football/ap/seasons.cfm?appollid=", appollid)
  # Invalid or missing pages return an empty tibble.
  page <- tryCatch(
    read_html(url),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  if (is.null(page)) {
    return(tibble())
  }
  
  Sys.sleep(0.5)
  
  poll_label <- page %>%
    html_element("h2") %>%
    html_text(trim = TRUE)
  
  rows <- page %>% html_elements("table tbody tr")
  
  map_df(rows, function(x) {
    rank_val <- x %>%
      html_element("td.text-end strong") %>%
      html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
      suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))
    
    team_val <- x %>%
      html_element("td a") %>%
      html_text(trim = TRUE)
    
    if (!is.na(rank_val) && !is.na(team_val) && rank_val >= 1 && rank_val <= 25) {
      tibble(
        rank = rank_val,
        team = team_val,
        year = year,
        poll_num = poll_num,
        appollid = appollid,
        poll_label = poll_label
      )
    } else {
      tibble()
    }
  })
}

if (!file.exists("data/ap_raw.rds")) {
  ap_raw <- map_df(1:nrow(poll_map), function(i) {
    yr <- poll_map$year[i]
    final_id <- poll_map$final_id[i]
    n_polls <- poll_map$n_polls[i]
    
    message("Scraping AP polls for year: ", yr)
    
    map_df(0:(n_polls - 1), function(w) {
      get_ap_poll(
        appollid = final_id - (n_polls - 1 - w),
        year = yr,
        poll_num = w + 1
      )
    })
  })
  
  saveRDS(ap_raw, "data/ap_raw.rds")
} else {
  ap_raw <- readRDS("data/ap_raw.rds")
}

ap_clean <- ap_raw %>%
  mutate(team_clean = clean_team(team))
```
```{r}
ap_clean <- ap_raw %>%
  mutate(
    team_clean = clean_team(team),
    rank = as.numeric(rank),
    poll_num = as.numeric(poll_num),
    year = as.numeric(year)
  )
# Convert ranking, poll number, and year fields to numeric.
# This avoids issues where scraped values are read as text instead of numbers.
```


```{r}
# 8. BUILD AP POLL FEATURES

ap_features <- ap_clean %>%
  group_by(team_clean, year) %>%
  summarise(
    times_ranked = n(),
    avg_rank = round(mean(rank, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    best_rank = min(rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    final_rank = rank[poll_num == max(poll_num)][1],
    preseason_rank = rank[poll_num == 1][1],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    final_rank = replace_na(as.numeric(final_rank), 26),
    preseason_rank = replace_na(as.numeric(preseason_rank), 26)
  )
```

```{r}
# 9. MERGE GAME RECORDS + RECRUITING + AP POLL FEATURES


# Merge game performance, recruiting, and AP poll features using cleaned team names and year.

final_df <- records_clean %>%
  left_join(recruiting_clean, by = c("team_clean", "year")) %>%
  left_join(ap_features, by = c("team_clean", "year"))

# Left joins keep all team-year game records even if recruiting or ranking data is missing.
```

```{r}
# 10. BUILD RECRUITING LAG FEATURES

final_df <- final_df %>%
  arrange(team_clean, year) %>%
  group_by(team_clean) %>%
  mutate(
    rec_rank_lag1 = lag(rec_rank, 1),
    rec_rank_lag2 = lag(rec_rank, 2),
    rec_rank_lag3 = lag(rec_rank, 3),
    
    rec_points_lag1 = lag(rec_points, 1),
    rec_points_lag2 = lag(rec_points, 2),
    rec_points_lag3 = lag(rec_points, 3),
    
    avg_rec_points_prev_3yrs = round(
      (rec_points_lag1 + rec_points_lag2 + rec_points_lag3) / 3,
      1
    ),
    
    avg_rec_rank_prev_3yrs = round(
      (rec_rank_lag1 + rec_rank_lag2 + rec_rank_lag3) / 3,
      1
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
# 11. DATA VALIDATION TABLE


# Validate the merged dataset for common data quality issues.
validation_table <- tibble(
  check_name = c(
    "Missing team names",
    "Impossible win percentage",
    "Wins greater than games",
    "Negative games",
    "Duplicate team-year rows",
    "Missing recruiting points after merge",
    "Missing AP ranking features after merge"
  ),
  failed_rows = c(
    sum(is.na(final_df$team) | final_df$team == ""),
    sum(final_df$win_pct < 0 | final_df$win_pct > 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(final_df$wins > final_df$games, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(final_df$games < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    final_df %>%
      count(team_clean, year) %>%
      filter(n > 1) %>%
      nrow(),
    sum(is.na(final_df$rec_points)),
    sum(is.na(final_df$times_ranked))
  )
)
#checks missing names, impossible values, duplicates
validation_table
```

```{r}
# 12. EXPLORATORY SUMMARIES

final_df %>%
  summarise(
    teams = n_distinct(team_clean),
    seasons = n_distinct(year),
    rows = n(),
    avg_win_pct = round(mean(win_pct, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    avg_rec_points = round(mean(rec_points, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  )

final_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(rec_points)) %>%
  summarise(
    correlation_rec_points_win_pct = round(cor(rec_points, win_pct, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    correlation_prev_3yr_rec_points_win_pct = round(cor(avg_rec_points_prev_3yrs, win_pct, use = "complete.obs"), 3)
  )
```
```{r}
# 13. FINAL DASHBOARD DATASET

# Keep only team-year rows that successfully matched recruiting data.

dashboard_df <- final_df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(rec_points)   # keeps teams that matched recruiting data
  ) %>%
  select(
    year,
    team,
    team_clean,
    games,
    wins,
    losses,
    win_pct,
    points_for_avg,
    points_against_avg,
    avg_pd,
    rec_rank,
    rec_points,
    rec_avg,
    five_star,
    four_star,
    three_star,
    commits,
    rec_rank_lag1,
    rec_rank_lag2,
    rec_rank_lag3,
    rec_points_lag1,
    rec_points_lag2,
    rec_points_lag3,
    avg_rec_points_prev_3yrs,
    avg_rec_rank_prev_3yrs,
    times_ranked,
    avg_rank,
    best_rank,
    final_rank,
    preseason_rank
  )

# Recruiting points are needed for the main dashboard analysis.
```

```{r}
dashboard_validation <- tibble(
  check_name = c(
    "Missing team names",
    "Impossible win percentage",
    "Wins greater than games",
    "Negative games",
    "Duplicate team-year rows",
    "Missing recruiting points",
    "Invalid AP ranking values"
  ),
  failed_rows = c(
    sum(is.na(dashboard_df$team) | dashboard_df$team == ""),
    sum(dashboard_df$win_pct < 0 | dashboard_df$win_pct > 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(dashboard_df$wins > dashboard_df$games, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(dashboard_df$games < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    dashboard_df %>%
      count(team_clean, year) %>%
      filter(n > 1) %>%
      nrow(),
    sum(is.na(dashboard_df$rec_points)),
    sum(dashboard_df$avg_rank < 1 | dashboard_df$avg_rank > 26, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

# Run a second validation pass on the final dashboard dataset.
# This confirms the exported file is clean

dashboard_validation
```

Missing AP rankings were not treated as errors. Teams not appearing in the AP poll were assigned a default rank of 26 and zero weeks ranked.

```{r}
dashboard_df <- dashboard_df %>%
  mutate(
    times_ranked = replace_na(times_ranked, 0),
    avg_rank = replace_na(avg_rank, 26),
    best_rank = replace_na(best_rank, 26),
    final_rank = replace_na(final_rank, 26),
    preseason_rank = replace_na(preseason_rank, 26)
  )
```


```{r}
# 14. EXPORT FINAL FILES


# Export the cleaned dashboard dataset and validation table for Tableau.
# These files are the final inputs used in the visualization portion of the project.

write.csv(dashboard_df, "data/final_college_football_recruiting_dashboard.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dashboard_validation, "data/dashboard_validation_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
